entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop studied, Arachis hypogaea L., evaluated for vegetative and reproductive growth.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental temperature regimes (22/18, 26/22, or 30/26°C) used to study growth effects.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD
entities.description: The duration of light exposure, tested as long day (12 h) or short day (9 h).

entities.name: TOTAL DRY MATTER PRODUCTION
entities.description: The total biomass produced by the plant.

entities.name: LEAF AREA
entities.description: The total surface area of the leaves.

entities.name: SHOOT AND ROOT DRY WEIGHT
entities.description: The combined dry mass of the plant's shoot and root systems.

entities.name: POD WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of the developed pods.

entities.name: POD NUMBERS
entities.description: The total count of pods produced.

entities.name: POD DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The biological process of pod formation and growth.

entities.name: CARBON ASSIMILATION
entities.description: The process by which the plant converts carbon dioxide into organic compounds.

entities.name: ROOT GROWTH
entities.description: The development and increase in mass of the root system.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The growth and development of the plant's reproductive organs.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE NODES
entities.description: Nodes on the plant that produce reproductive structures.

entities.name: VEGETATIVE NODES
entities.description: Nodes on the plant that produce vegetative structures.

entities.name: POD TO PEG RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of pods to pegs, used as an indicator of photoperiod sensitivity.

entities.name: GENOTYPIC SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The degree to which different genotypes respond to environmental factors like photoperiod.

entities.name: CONTROLLED-ENVIRONMENT GROWTH CHAMBERS
entities.description: The experimental setup used to maintain specific temperature and photoperiod regimes.

relationships.description: Affects the vegetative and reproductive growth of the crop
Affects the vegetative and reproductive growth of the crop
Significant interaction effect on biomass production
Higher production observed under long day conditions
Greater leaf area observed under long day conditions
Accounted for 76% of the variation in dry weight
Lack of relationship between leaf area and pod weight
Lack of relationship between leaf area and pod numbers
Controlled by factors other than carbon assimilation
Interaction with photoperiod affects root weight
Interaction with temperature affects root weight
Low temperature reduces the proportion of reproductive nodes
Low temperature reduces the proportion of reproductive nodes in total nodes
Lower ratio observed in long day than short day conditions
Used as an indicator of genotypic sensitivity to photoperiod
Used to grow cultivars under controlled temperature and photoperiod
